dict.string:
Self-Improving Log — the self-improving-skill loop (clawhub / Hermes style), as a Shortcut.
THE MECHANISM (there's no magic — it's a disciplined append-and-review loop over files): 1. CAPTURE → sort each note into one of three buckets: Error · Learning · Feature idea, then append a timestamped line to your "Self-Improvement Log" note. 2. REVIEW → re-read the log weekly (pair it with the PI Weekly Briefing shortcut) so you stop repeating the same mistakes. 3. PROMOTE → graduate durable lessons into your permanent operating principles / CLAUDE.md.
HONEST BOUNDARY: a Shortcut cannot rewrite its own actions or "learn" autonomously — that's an agent's job. This is the CAPTURE tool that feeds the loop, which is exactly what the clawhub/Hermes self-improving skills are too (they append to ERRORS/LEARNINGS/FEATURE files; they don't rewrite themselves either).
SETUP: in Choose from Menu, wire each branch to append to its bucket (Errors / Learnings / Features) note, or keep one "Self-Improvement Log" note tagged by bucket. Add to Siri ("log a learning") and the Share Sheet for capture-anywhere. 100% device-local, no network.
Describe it (a mistake, a fix, a better way):
📝 Captured. Review it weekly. Future-you says thanks.
